news_list.title: West Asia crisis: LPG imports run out of gas despite increase in supplies from US, Iran
news_list.summary:
New Delhi: India's LPG imports in March fell more than 40% compared with the previous two months, opening up a significant supply gap despite increased imports from the US and Iran, show ship-tracking data.

In a twist, the US and Iran -- the two countries whose conflict has disrupted LPG flows to India -- helped partly plug the shortfall.

The US emerged as India's largest supplier of the fuel in March, while Iran resumed shipments after a gap of nearly seven years.

Also Read: India attends UK-led talks as 37 countries sign pledge to secure Strait of Hormuz

India's total LPG imports dropped to about 1.22 million tonnes in March, a 46% fall compared with January and 40% lower than in February, a 28-day month, according to ship-tracker Kpler.

Supplies have been affected since February 28, when the Iran war began, resulting in maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

The West Asia war has led to disruptions in maritime traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

Catch all war-related developments here

"Roughly a third of global LPG exports originate from the Mideast Gulf and transit through the Strait of Hormuz," said Nikhil Dubey, senior research analyst at Kpler. "Even if the situation stabilises, restoring supply chains and normalising logistics will take time, likely keeping international LPG prices elevated in the near to medium term."

The UAE, Qatar, Kuwait and Saudi Arabia -- traditionally India's top four suppliers -- shipped just 672,000 tonnes, or 55%, of India's total arrivals in March. That is about 36% of their January and roughly 40% of February shipments to India. Supplies from the UAE, typically India's largest source, fell to 226,000 tonnes, or 28% of its January levels.

The US supplied the most at 420,000 tonnes in March, up 30% from February and 56% higher than January. Iran supplied 43,000 tonnes compared with no shipments in January and February. Smaller volumes also arrived from Argentina and Malaysia.

With imports meeting about 60% of India's LPG consumption, the sharp drop in inbound supplies created a shortfall. A roughly 40% ramp-up in domestic production by mid-March could only partly offset that deficit.

DOMESTIC DEAMND PICKS UP

Last month, the oil ministry asked refiners to divert certain hydrocarbon streams towards LPG production, away from petrochemicals, to boost domestic supply. It has now begun partly reversing the move as demand from pharmaceuticals, food, chemicals and petrochemicals sectors picks up.

news_list.title: South Korea CubeSat begins radiation mission on Artemis 2 - UPI.com
news_list.summary:
April 2 (Asia Today) -- South Korea's CubeSat K-RadCube has begun operations in high Earth orbit after being deployed from NASA's Artemis 2 mission, marking a step toward developing capabilities for future crewed spaceflight.

The satellite was launched aboard the Artemis 2 crewed lunar mission from the Kennedy Space Center in Florida and successfully established communication after deployment, South Korea's space agency said Wednesday.

K-RadCube was released into orbit at an altitude of about 40,000 kilometers and is currently undergoing initial stabilization. Authorities said communication may remain intermittent during the early phase and plan to conduct intensive monitoring over the next two days.

The satellite's primary mission is to measure space radiation across different altitudes within the Van Allen radiation belts, a region surrounding Earth where astronauts can be exposed to high levels of radiation during deep-space travel.

Related Satellite images suggest North Korea warship nearing launch Seoul stocks drop nearly 4.5 pct amid dashed hope for swift end to Middle East war South Korea to fully open DMZ peace trail routes

Data collected from the mission will be used to analyze how space radiation affects astronauts traveling between Earth and the Moon, a key challenge for long-duration crewed missions.

The project was led by the Korea Astronomy and Space Science Institute, with participation from private companies including Nara Space, Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix in design and manufacturing.

K-RadCube is also equipped with next-generation multi-chip modules and memory semiconductors to test the durability and performance of electronic components in a radiation environment.

Officials said the mission helps establish the foundation for South Korea to study the effects of space conditions on the human body, as global space powers accelerate plans for crewed missions to the Moon and Mars.

Moon Hong-kyu, a principal researcher at the institute, said space radiation remains one of the biggest obstacles to human exploration beyond Earth.

"K-RadCube represents Korea entering a stage where it can contribute to setting technical standards and safety benchmarks for the era of crewed space exploration," he said.

-- Reported by Asia Today; translated by UPI

© Asia Today. Unauthorized reproduction or redistribution prohibited.

Original Korean report: https://www.asiatoday.co.kr/kn/view.php?key=20260402010000819

news_list.title: Thailand Employs AI To Revolutionize The Payment Industry
news_list.summary:
Picture this: You're a busy shopper rushing through Chatuchak Market in Bangkok, Thailand. You scan a QR code at a street food stall, pay in seconds, and walk away without a worry. AI works behind the scenes to make that payment instant, secure, and tailored just for you.

Thailand's payment scene has exploded. PromptPay now boasts over 90 million users, handling more than 74 million transactions daily as of June 2025. That's in a country of about 70 million people, where 92% of folks use digital payments. Fintech grows at 12.8% yearly, fueled by mobile banking and e-wallets like TrueMoney and ShopeePay.

AI revolutionizing payments in Thailand takes this further. It speeds up approvals by spotting patterns in real time, so your transfer flies through without delays. It boosts safety through smarter fraud detection, blocking scams before they hit. Plus, it personalizes offers, like suggesting the best wallet based on your habits.

This post breaks it down for you. You'll see how AI transforms fraud detection, customizes experiences, spotlights key players like the Bank of Thailand, and shapes future trends. Stick around to learn practical ways it keeps your money safer in Thailand's booming payment world.

Thailand ditched cash fast. People now grab smartphones for payments everywhere, from street vendors to shiny malls. PromptPay leads this charge. It claims 41% of mobile payments and 44% of e-commerce deals. Thai QR codes connect banks, wallets, and even Alipay for tourists. No wonder 61.5% of Thais scan QR codes monthly; that's the third highest worldwide. Online payments grew 13.5% yearly, pushing digital sales to $56 billion in 2025, with more ahead in 2026.

This boom creates headaches, too. Fraud spikes as volumes soar. Services sometimes lag during peaks. AI steps in here. It spots risks instantly and smooths transactions. Result? Safer, quicker payments that match the pace.

PromptPay changed everything. Over 90 million users registered by mid-2025, up 14% from last year. It handles 74 million transactions daily, powering buys from coffee to rent.

You link your phone number or national ID to bank accounts. Transfers happen in seconds, often free. The Bank of Thailand (BOT) ensures QR codes work across banks. That means one scan settles any bill.

Picture a Chiang Mai night market. You order spicy som tam. The vendor shows a QR code. Your app beeps; payment done. No cash fumbling. Check out how PromptPay reshapes Thailand's payments. Every day of life runs more smoothly.

Digital wallets exploded alongside PromptPay. TrueMoney and ShopeePay thrive on real-time transfers. E-commerce surges because shoppers pay instantly from apps.

Over 150,000 businesses already use AI tools for payments. Government agency DEPA trains small shops on wallets. They add QR setups cheaply and quickly.

AI supercharges this. It predicts spending habits, cuts fraud, and speeds approvals. Vendors at markets or malls get cash flow without delays. Tourists link Alipay seamlessly. Growth hits new highs, but AI keeps it safe. Small shops compete better now.

Fraud tops the list for AI in payments. In fact, 63.5% of APAC fintech leaders rank it as their main focus. Thailand sees why. June 2025 brought 24,500 scam cases alone, with losses hitting 2.8 billion baht. Old methods relied on basic rules, like fixed spending limits. They missed clever tricks. AI changes that. It scans patterns live, predicts risks with agentic systems, and uses digital IDs for quick checks. The Bank of Thailand pushes these tools hard. Result? You pay at a market stall, and scams stop cold. Feel safer yet?

AI watches every PromptPay scan right then. It pulls risk data from global feeds and local banks. Spot an odd buy? It flags it mid-transaction.

For example, you try a 50,000 baht transfer from Bangkok to an unknown account at 2 a.m. AI compares your habits. Too big, too late? It pauses and asks for extra proof, like a fingerprint. Old systems waited for reports after the fact. Now, blocks happen in seconds.

Banks shut 3 million fraud-linked accounts by July 2025. BOT added daily caps, too. AI powers this speed. During peaks, it handles millions of checks without slowing your pad thai payment.

See Thailand's new 200 million baht AI system against online fraud. Scammers hate it.

AI builds a profile of you over time. It tracks safe patterns, like your weekly market spends or travel buys. Then it spots breaks.

You always pay small amounts locally. Suddenly, a big overseas wire? AI alerts right away. It learns from past data without storing extras, thanks to PDPA rules. Those protect your info; firms anonymize habits first.

Agentic AI goes further. It predicts risks by simulating scams. In Thailand, deepfakes and fake QR codes rose fast. Check AI deepfake scams targeting Thai working adults. Behavioral checks caught many in 2025.

Old alerts missed these shifts. AI adapts daily. You get peace; your wallet stays full. Banks report fewer losses already.

AI turns generic payments into something that feels made just for you. It predicts what you need before you ask. Banks in Thailand use it to suggest deals or handle bills automatically. This personalization saves time and money. Plus, richer data from ISO 20022 standards lets AI work smarter. Adoption jumped 32% in Thai fintech last year. Now payments match your life perfectly.

AI guesses your needs by watching spending patterns. It spots when you buy groceries or travel. Then it pushes custom deals right to your app. For example, Siam Commercial Bank (SCB) launched EASY Store. This AI tool scans your habits and offers tailored loans, cards, or insurance. Imagine shopping at a Bangkok market. Your SCB app pings a discount on nearby som tam because you often grab street food there.

Kasikornbank does this too. Their predictive AI forecasts bills like rent or utilities. It suggests auto-payments or cashback boosts. ISO 20022 helps here. It shares detailed transaction data across banks. So AI pulls precise info for better predictions.

These offers cut costs. You save on fees or get exclusive perks. Check SCB EASY's hyper-personalized AI store for real examples. In short, AI makes banking feel like a helpful friend.

Agentic AI takes over routine tasks. It runs your accounts, pays bills, and schedules transfers without you lifting a finger. Picture an AI agent checking your TrueMoney wallet. It spots an upcoming phone bill and pays it via PromptPay. No apps to open, no delays.

In Thailand, banks build these agents fast. They automate invoices and flag low balances. This frees you for bigger things. Benefits stack up: less hassle, fewer errors, real savings. The future looks bright. E-commerce could hit 1.8 trillion baht by 2030, with AI agents driving 15-25% more automated buys. Shoppers let agents handle carts and payments. See Thai e-commerce growth to 2030.

Meanwhile, agentic systems link wallets across borders. They speed up payouts for freelancers. Read about agentic AI in Thai banking operations. You get smooth, smart payments every day.

Thailand's payment leaders blend central bank oversight with private sector speed. No one company dominates. Instead, the Bank of Thailand (BOT) sets the rules while banks and fintechs build on them. They focus on AI for secure links across borders and daily use. This teamwork makes payments faster and safer for everyone.

The BOT drives big changes in payments. It pushes interoperability so systems talk easily. PromptPay QR codes now work nationwide, and AI checks them in real time.

Cross-border links grow fast too. BOT leads Project Nexus with ASEAN partners like India and Malaysia. This will connect instant payments across borders by 2026. Plus, Thailand launched QR payments with China in October 2025. Chinese tourists scan to pay in RMB via Alipay or WeChat at Thai shops. See the BOT announcement on Thailand-China QR linkage.

Standards like ISO 20022 help AI analyze data better. BOT's AI risk guidelines from 2025 require banks to test systems for bias and security. Result? Safer transfers that handle peaks without fails.

DEPA supports this by training small businesses on these tools.

Banks and fintechs partner for quick wins. They build Banking as a Service (BaaS) platforms. Kasikornbank teams with startups for AI-driven apps that link wallets seamlessly.

These groups create hyper-connected payments. AI agents move money across apps without hiccups. For example, TrueMoney integrates with bank APIs for instant transfers.

Fintechs shine at events like Money20/20 Asia in Bangkok. Leaders from SCB and ShopeePay discuss AI fraud tools there. No single winner emerges. Everyone collaborates because competition hurts growth.

DEPA helps small shops join in. It offers free AI training for QR setups and fraud alerts. Banks provide the tech backbone. Together, they serve tourists and locals alike. You benefit from faster, smarter options every day. Check AI advancements in Thai finance for more on these shifts.

AI will push Thailand's payments even further. Cross-border links grow fast, especially with ASEAN neighbors and China. New rules balance speed and safety. Meanwhile, cyber risks loom, but smart tech fights back. Your transfers stay quick and secure as the digital economy hits $56 billion GMV.

Thailand connects payments across borders now. BOT rolled out real-time QR links in China in early 2026. Chinese tourists scan Alipay or WeChat at Thai shops and pay in RMB instantly. Thais do the same in China with PromptPay.

ASEAN ties strengthen, too. PromptPay hooks up with Singapore's PayNow and Malaysia's DuitNow. Transfers zip in seconds, no high fees. Project Nexus aims for full rollout by late 2026. AI predicts flows, unifies old systems, and spots issues live.

Non-banks join in with licenses for e-money abroad. TouristDigiPay tests crypto swaps to baht wallets. AI expansion forecasts 20% growth in cross-border volume. Legacy banks integrate smoothly, so you send money home from Laos without hassle. Check the Thailand PromptPay Cross Border 2026 guide for details.

New AI rules kick in 2026. A risk-based framework targets high-stakes uses like payment decisions. ETDA leads with sector-specific duties from the BOT and SEC. Businesses map AI risks now to avoid fines.

PDPA enforces privacy hard. Firms anonymize data, get consent, and limit storage. AI models follow these in training and use. See Thailand's new AI laws 2026 guide for compliance steps.

Cyber threats rise with more links. Hacks target fragmented systems, but AI spots them first. PromptPay earned SIRPS status in March 2026, so it gets top safeguards. ISO 20022 standards help too.

Governance centers oversee it all. Challenges persist, yet AI tools predict attacks. In short, regs build trust. Payments grow safer, and Thailand leads ASEAN. You win with reliable, border-free cash moves.

AI boosts safety in Thailand's payments first. It spots fraud in real time, so PromptPay scans stay secure during night market buys. Banks cut scams fast, thanks to tools from the Bank of Thailand.

Next, AI adds personalization that fits your life. It suggests deals on TrueMoney or auto-pays bills via agentic systems. In addition, innovation links borders, like PromptPay with PayNow, for quick ASEAN transfers.

Try PromptPay apps with AI features today. They make payments smoother and safer. Also, follow the Bank of Thailand updates for the latest.

Picture Thailand fully cashless by 2027. Everyone scans QR codes without worry. AI powers smart, instant money moves everywhere. You lead that shift now.
news_list.source: CTN News l Chiang Rai Times

news_list.title: 'Many jump ship for better wages'
news_list.summary:
PETALING JAYA: Many Malaysian seafarers are jumping ship. And that's because the wages being offered - from ratings (crew) to senior officers - trail way behind what others offer, says the Association of Malaysian Maritime Professionals (Ikmal).

Its president, Capt Abdul Aziz Abdullah said many Malaysian senior officers in the deck and engine departments are currently serving on foreign-registered ships due to wage disparities at home.

He said wages must be urgently re-examined to bring them on par with shore-based professions and international shipping standards.

The Malaysia Marine Department, he said, has conducted a study on wage structures and will soon publish recommended rates for shipping companies.

"This wage anomaly has resulted in many of our experienced officers opting to work on foreign-flagged vessels, where the perks and benefits are more competitive," he said yesterday.

Capt Abdul Aziz said there were also other structural challenges.

These include contractual employment, which has replaced the permanent tenure enjoyed by earlier generations of seafarers.

"In the past, seafarers were permanently employed, received pay even while on leave, and were granted study leave to pursue mandatory upgrading courses," he added.

Today, most are hired on fixed-term contracts. Once they sign off at the end of a voyage, their contractual ties with the company end until they are re-engaged.

"This leads to irregular income streams and makes it difficult to manage long-term financial commitments," he added.

The maritime expert said the financial burden was compounded by mandatory training and certification requirements by the International Maritime Organisation.

"Seafarers have to undergo upgrading and revalidation courses at their own expense, and course fees keep rising," he added.

Capt Abdul Aziz also highlighted a taxation anomaly. Seafarers on ocean-going vessels on unlimited voyages are exempt from personal income tax but those serving onboard offshore support vessels are not.

The profession has also become increasingly demanding, with reduced crew sizes, heightened regulatory requirements and growing commercial pressures contributing to fatigue.

Ikmal also expressed concern over the substantial presence of non-Malaysian seafarers on Malaysian-flagged ships and wage disparities across nationalities.

This has led to fewer young Malaysians entering the profession.

"Without meaningful and collaborative reforms, the industry risks continued attrition of professionals and a weakening pipeline of future maritime talent." Capt Abdul Aziz said.

themes.name: 中東局勢對美元及市場情緒的影響
themes.insight: 多篇報告（MoneyDJ）顯示，中東局勢的變化，無論是緊張升級或緩和降溫，都直接影響美元的震盪。局勢升級推動風險貨幣下挫，而局勢降溫則可能導致美元下跌。這表明中東地緣政治風險是當前市場情緒和貨幣走勢的關鍵驅動因素。
evidence: 聚焦川普講話，美元上下震盪
中東局勢降溫，引發美元下挫
中東局勢不確定 黃金產業仍具
中東衝突升級，風險貨幣下挫

themes.name: AI在泰國支付行業的應用與發展
themes.insight: 泰國正積極利用人工智慧（AI）革新其支付行業。AI的應用涵蓋了即時交易處理、增強的欺詐偵測、個人化金融服務以及代理AI在自動化支付任務中的應用。同時，泰國央行正推動標準化和跨境支付的互聯互通，並加強AI的監管與隱私保護。
sources: CTN News l Chiang Rai Times
evidence: Thailand Employs AI To Revolutionize The Payment Industry

themes.name: 馬來西亞海事專業人士面臨薪資與合約挑戰
themes.insight: 馬來西亞海事專業人士協會（Ikmal）指出，馬來西亞海員的薪資遠低於國際標準，導致許多經驗豐富的專業人士轉向外國船隻工作。此外，合約制取代永久聘用、不斷上漲的培訓費用以及稅務問題，都對海員的收入和職業發展造成了負面影響。
evidence: 'Many jump ship for better wages'

themes.name: 川普言論對美元匯率的影響
themes.insight: 美國前總統川普的講話正對美元匯率產生顯著影響，導致美元上下震盪。市場正密切關注其動態，這顯示了政治人物的言論在當前市場環境中的重要性。
evidence: 聚焦川普講話，美元上下震盪

themes.name: 南韓CubeSat的太空輻射監測任務
themes.insight: 南韓的K-RadCube立方衛星已成功部署並開始在高地球軌道進行太空輻射測量任務。該衛星的目標是分析輻射對太空人往返月球過程中的影響，並測試電子元件在輻射環境下的性能，為南韓未來的載人太空探索奠定基礎。
evidence: South Korea CubeSat begins radiation mission on Artemis 2 - UPI.com

items.title: South Korea CubeSat begins radiation mission on Artemis 2 - UPI.com
items.summary:

Data collected from the mission will be used to analyze how space radiation affects astronauts traveling between Earth and the Moon, a key challenge for long-duration crewed missions.

K-RadCube is also equipped with next-generation multi-chip modules and memory semiconductors to test the durability and performance of electronic components in a radiation environment.

Officials said the mission helps establish the foundation for South Korea to study the effects of space conditions on the human body, as global space powers accelerate plans for crewed missions to the Moon and Mars.

Moon Hong-kyu, a principal researcher at the institute, said space radiation remains one of the biggest obstacles to human exploration beyond Earth.

"K-RadCube represents Korea entering a stage where it can contribute to setting technical standards and safety benchmarks for the era of crewed space exploration," he said.

-- Reported by Asia Today; translated by UPI

© Asia Today. Unauthorized reproduction or redistribution prohibited.

Original Korean report: https://www.asiatoday.co.kr/kn/view.php?key=20260402010000819

items.title: Thailand Employs AI To Revolutionize The Payment Industry
items.summary:
Picture this: You're a busy shopper rushing through Chatuchak Market in Bangkok, Thailand. You scan a QR code at a street food stall, pay in seconds, and walk away without a worry. AI works behind the scenes to make that payment instant, secure, and tailored just for you.

Thailand's payment scene has exploded. PromptPay now boasts over 90 million users, handling more than 74 million transactions daily as of June 2025. That's in a country of about 70 million people, where 92% of folks use digital payments. Fintech grows at 12.8% yearly, fueled by mobile banking and e-wallets like TrueMoney and ShopeePay.

AI revolutionizing payments in Thailand takes this further. It speeds up approvals by spotting patterns in real time, so your transfer flies through without delays. It boosts safety through smarter fraud detection, blocking scams before they hit. Plus, it personalizes offers, like suggesting the best wallet based on your habits.

This post breaks it down for you. You'll see how AI transforms fraud detection, customizes experiences, spotlights key players like the Bank of Thailand, and shapes future trends. Stick around to learn practical ways it keeps your money safer in Thailand's booming payment world.

Thailand ditched cash fast. People now grab smartphones for payments everywhere, from street vendors to shiny malls. PromptPay leads this charge. It claims 41% of mobile payments and 44% of e-commerce deals. Thai QR codes connect banks, wallets, and even Alipay for tourists. No wonder 61.5% of Thais scan QR codes monthly; that's the third highest worldwide. Online payments grew 13.5% yearly, pushing digital sales to $56 billion in 2025, with more ahead in 2026.

This boom creates headaches, too. Fraud spikes as volumes soar. Services sometimes lag during peaks. AI steps in here. It spots risks instantly and smooths transactions. Result? Safer, quicker payments that match the pace.

PromptPay changed everything. Over 90 million users registered by mid-2025, up 14% from last year. It handles 74 million transactions daily, powering buys from coffee to rent.

You link your phone number or national ID to bank accounts. Transfers happen in seconds, often free. The Bank of Thailand (BOT) ensures QR codes work across banks. That means one scan settles any bill.

Picture a Chiang Mai night market. You order spicy som tam. The vendor shows a QR code. Your app beeps; payment done. No cash fumbling. Check out how PromptPay reshapes Thailand's payments. Every day of life runs more smoothly.

Digital wallets exploded alongside PromptPay. TrueMoney and ShopeePay thrive on real-time transfers. E-commerce surges because shoppers pay instantly from apps.

Over 150,000 businesses already use AI tools for payments. Government agency DEPA trains small shops on wallets. They add QR setups cheaply and quickly.

AI supercharges this. It predicts spending habits, cuts fraud, and speeds approvals. Vendors at markets or malls get cash flow without delays. Tourists link Alipay seamlessly. Growth hits new highs, but AI keeps it safe. Small shops compete better now.

Fraud tops the list for AI in payments. In fact, 63.5% of APAC fintech leaders rank it as their main focus. Thailand sees why. June 2025 brought 24,500 scam cases alone, with losses hitting 2.8 billion baht. Old methods relied on basic rules, like fixed spending limits. They missed clever tricks. AI changes that. It scans patterns live, predicts risks with agentic systems, and uses digital IDs for quick checks. The Bank of Thailand pushes these tools hard. Result? You pay at a market stall, and scams stop cold. Feel safer yet?

AI watches every PromptPay scan right then. It pulls risk data from global feeds and local banks. Spot an odd buy? It flags it mid-transaction.

For example, you try a 50,000 baht transfer from Bangkok to an unknown account at 2 a.m. AI compares your habits. Too big, too late? It pauses and asks for extra proof, like a fingerprint. Old systems waited for reports after the fact. Now, blocks happen in seconds.

Banks shut 3 million fraud-linked accounts by July 2025. BOT added daily caps, too. AI powers this speed. During peaks, it handles millions of checks without slowing your pad thai payment.

See Thailand's new 200 million baht AI system against online fraud. Scammers hate it.

AI builds a profile of you over time. It tracks safe patterns, like your weekly market spends or travel buys. Then it spots breaks.

You always pay small amounts locally. Suddenly, a big overseas wire? AI alerts right away. It learns from past data without storing extras, thanks to PDPA rules. Those protect your info; firms anonymize habits first.

Agentic AI goes further. It predicts risks by simulating scams. In Thailand, deepfakes and fake QR codes rose fast. Check AI deepfake scams targeting Thai working adults. Behavioral checks caught many in 2025.

Old alerts missed these shifts. AI adapts daily. You get peace; your wallet stays full. Banks report fewer losses already.

AI turns generic payments into something that feels made just for you. It predicts what you need before you ask. Banks in Thailand use it to suggest deals or handle bills automatically. This personalization saves time and money. Plus, richer data from ISO 20022 standards lets AI work smarter. Adoption jumped 32% in Thai fintech last year. Now payments match your life perfectly.

AI guesses your needs by watching spending patterns. It spots when you buy groceries or travel. Then it pushes custom deals right to your app. For example, Siam Commercial Bank (SCB) launched EASY Store. This AI tool scans your habits and offers tailored loans, cards, or insurance. Imagine shopping at a Bangkok market. Your SCB app pings a discount on nearby som tam because you often grab street food there.

Kasikornbank does this too. Their predictive AI forecasts bills like rent or utilities. It suggests auto-payments or cashback boosts. ISO 20022 helps here. It shares detailed transaction data across banks. So AI pulls precise info for better predictions.

These offers cut costs. You save on fees or get exclusive perks. Check SCB EASY's hyper-personalized AI store for real examples. In short, AI makes banking feel like a helpful friend.

Agentic AI takes over routine tasks. It runs your accounts, pays bills, and schedules transfers without you lifting a finger. Picture an AI agent checking your TrueMoney wallet. It spots an upcoming phone bill and pays it via PromptPay. No apps to open, no delays.

In Thailand, banks build these agents fast. They automate invoices and flag low balances. This frees you for bigger things. Benefits stack up: less hassle, fewer errors, real savings. The future looks bright. E-commerce could hit 1.8 trillion baht by 2030, with AI agents driving 15-25% more automated buys. Shoppers let agents handle carts and payments. See Thai e-commerce growth to 2030.

Meanwhile, agentic systems link wallets across borders. They speed up payouts for freelancers. Read about agentic AI in Thai banking operations. You get smooth, smart payments every day.

Thailand's payment leaders blend central bank oversight with private sector speed. No one company dominates. Instead, the Bank of Thailand (BOT) sets the rules while banks and fintechs build on them. They focus on AI for secure links across borders and daily use. This teamwork makes payments faster and safer for everyone.

The BOT drives big changes in payments. It pushes interoperability so systems talk easily. PromptPay QR codes now work nationwide, and AI checks them in real time.

Cross-border links grow fast too. BOT leads Project Nexus with ASEAN partners like India and Malaysia. This will connect instant payments across borders by 2026. Plus, Thailand launched QR payments with China in October 2025. Chinese tourists scan to pay in RMB via Alipay or WeChat at Thai shops. See the BOT announcement on Thailand-China QR linkage.

Standards like ISO 20022 help AI analyze data better. BOT's AI risk guidelines from 2025 require banks to test systems for bias and security. Result? Safer transfers that handle peaks without fails.

DEPA supports this by training small businesses on these tools.

Banks and fintechs partner for quick wins. They build Banking as a Service (BaaS) platforms. Kasikornbank teams with startups for AI-driven apps that link wallets seamlessly.

These groups create hyper-connected payments. AI agents move money across apps without hiccups. For example, TrueMoney integrates with bank APIs for instant transfers.

Fintechs shine at events like Money20/20 Asia in Bangkok. Leaders from SCB and ShopeePay discuss AI fraud tools there. No single winner emerges. Everyone collaborates because competition hurts growth.

DEPA helps small shops join in. It offers free AI training for QR setups and fraud alerts. Banks provide the tech backbone. Together, they serve tourists and locals alike. You benefit from faster, smarter options every day. Check AI advancements in Thai finance for more on these shifts.

AI will push Thailand's payments even further. Cross-border links grow fast, especially with ASEAN neighbors and China. New rules balance speed and safety. Meanwhile, cyber risks loom, but smart tech fights back. Your transfers stay quick and secure as the digital economy hits $56 billion GMV.

Thailand connects payments across borders now. BOT rolled out real-time QR links in China in early 2026. Chinese tourists scan Alipay or WeChat at Thai shops and pay in RMB instantly. Thais do the same in China with PromptPay.

ASEAN ties strengthen, too. PromptPay hooks up with Singapore's PayNow and Malaysia's DuitNow. Transfers zip in seconds, no high fees. Project Nexus aims for full rollout by late 2026. AI predicts flows, unifies old systems, and spots issues live.

Non-banks join in with licenses for e-money abroad. TouristDigiPay tests crypto swaps to baht wallets. AI expansion forecasts 20% growth in cross-border volume. Legacy banks integrate smoothly, so you send money home from Laos without hassle. Check the Thailand PromptPay Cross Border 2026 guide for details.

New AI rules kick in 2026. A risk-based framework targets high-stakes uses like payment decisions. ETDA leads with sector-specific duties from the BOT and SEC. Businesses map AI risks now to avoid fines.

PDPA enforces privacy hard. Firms anonymize data, get consent, and limit storage. AI models follow these in training and use. See Thailand's new AI laws 2026 guide for compliance steps.

Cyber threats rise with more links. Hacks target fragmented systems, but AI spots them first. PromptPay earned SIRPS status in March 2026, so it gets top safeguards. ISO 20022 standards help too.

Governance centers oversee it all. Challenges persist, yet AI tools predict attacks. In short, regs build trust. Payments grow safer, and Thailand leads ASEAN. You win with reliable, border-free cash moves.

AI boosts safety in Thailand's payments first. It spots fraud in real time, so PromptPay scans stay secure during night market buys. Banks cut scams fast, thanks to tools from the Bank of Thailand.

Next, AI adds personalization that fits your life. It suggests deals on TrueMoney or auto-pays bills via agentic systems. In addition, innovation links borders, like PromptPay with PayNow, for quick ASEAN transfers.

Try PromptPay apps with AI features today. They make payments smoother and safer. Also, follow the Bank of Thailand updates for the latest.

Picture Thailand fully cashless by 2027. Everyone scans QR codes without worry. AI powers smart, instant money moves everywhere. You lead that shift now.
items.source: CTN News l Chiang Rai Times

items.title: 'Many jump ship for better wages'
items.summary:
PETALING JAYA: Many Malaysian seafarers are jumping ship. And that's because the wages being offered - from ratings (crew) to senior officers - trail way behind what others offer, says the Association of Malaysian Maritime Professionals (Ikmal).

Its president, Capt Abdul Aziz Abdullah said many Malaysian senior officers in the deck and engine departments are currently serving on foreign-registered ships due to wage disparities at home.

He said wages must be urgently re-examined to bring them on par with shore-based professions and international shipping standards.

The Malaysia Marine Department, he said, has conducted a study on wage structures and will soon publish recommended rates for shipping companies.

"This wage anomaly has resulted in many of our experienced officers opting to work on foreign-flagged vessels, where the perks and benefits are more competitive," he said yesterday.

Capt Abdul Aziz said there were also other structural challenges.

These include contractual employment, which has replaced the permanent tenure enjoyed by earlier generations of seafarers.

"In the past, seafarers were permanently employed, received pay even while on leave, and were granted study leave to pursue mandatory upgrading courses," he added.

Today, most are hired on fixed-term contracts. Once they sign off at the end of a voyage, their contractual ties with the company end until they are re-engaged.

"This leads to irregular income streams and makes it difficult to manage long-term financial commitments," he added.

The maritime expert said the financial burden was compounded by mandatory training and certification requirements by the International Maritime Organisation.

"Seafarers have to undergo upgrading and revalidation courses at their own expense, and course fees keep rising," he added.

Capt Abdul Aziz also highlighted a taxation anomaly. Seafarers on ocean-going vessels on unlimited voyages are exempt from personal income tax but those serving onboard offshore support vessels are not.

The profession has also become increasingly demanding, with reduced crew sizes, heightened regulatory requirements and growing commercial pressures contributing to fatigue.

Ikmal also expressed concern over the substantial presence of non-Malaysian seafarers on Malaysian-flagged ships and wage disparities across nationalities.

This has led to fewer young Malaysians entering the profession.

"Without meaningful and collaborative reforms, the industry risks continued attrition of professionals and a weakening pipeline of future maritime talent." Capt Abdul Aziz said.

topics.topic: 中東局勢對美元及市場情緒的影響
topics.summary: 多篇報告（MoneyDJ）顯示，中東局勢的變化，無論是緊張升級或緩和降溫，都直接影響美元的震盪。局勢升級推動風險貨幣下挫，而局勢降溫則可能導致美元下跌。這表明中東地緣政治風險是當前市場情緒和貨幣走勢的關鍵驅動因素。
evidence: 聚焦川普講話，美元上下震盪
中東局勢降溫，引發美元下挫
中東局勢不確定 黃金產業仍具
中東衝突升級，風險貨幣下挫

topics.topic: AI在泰國支付行業的應用與發展
topics.summary: 泰國正積極利用人工智慧（AI）革新其支付行業。AI的應用涵蓋了即時交易處理、增強的欺詐偵測、個人化金融服務以及代理AI在自動化支付任務中的應用。同時，泰國央行正推動標準化和跨境支付的互聯互通，並加強AI的監管與隱私保護。
sources: CTN News l Chiang Rai Times
evidence: Thailand Employs AI To Revolutionize The Payment Industry

topics.topic: 馬來西亞海事專業人士面臨薪資與合約挑戰
topics.summary: 馬來西亞海事專業人士協會（Ikmal）指出，馬來西亞海員的薪資遠低於國際標準，導致許多經驗豐富的專業人士轉向外國船隻工作。此外，合約制取代永久聘用、不斷上漲的培訓費用以及稅務問題，都對海員的收入和職業發展造成了負面影響。
evidence: 'Many jump ship for better wages'

topics.topic: 川普言論對美元匯率的影響
topics.summary: 美國前總統川普的講話正對美元匯率產生顯著影響，導致美元上下震盪。市場正密切關注其動態，這顯示了政治人物的言論在當前市場環境中的重要性。
evidence: 聚焦川普講話，美元上下震盪

topics.topic: 南韓CubeSat的太空輻射監測任務
topics.summary: 南韓的K-RadCube立方衛星已成功部署並開始在高地球軌道進行太空輻射測量任務。該衛星的目標是分析輻射對太空人往返月球過程中的影響，並測試電子元件在輻射環境下的性能，為南韓未來的載人太空探索奠定基礎。
evidence: South Korea CubeSat begins radiation mission on Artemis 2 - UPI.com

ai_analysis.full_text:
【今日結論】
今晚美股即將開盤，台指夜盤多空消息交錯，加上台積電ADR的下跌，預期週二台股開盤可能偏弱或以盤整為主，投資人應謹慎操作，逢高減碼並避開原物料相關標的。
【新聞驅動】
近期市場關注焦點圍繞在中東地緣政治引發的油價飆升，以及川普的關稅政策帶來的「美國例外論」的重新審視。同時，特斯拉交車不如預期和SpaceX IPO傳聞也為市場增添了情緒波動，顯示出市場在避險與投機之間搖擺。
【國際與台股】
儘管隔夜美股小幅收漲，但CNN恐懼貪婪指數處於極度恐懼區間，加上布蘭特原油飆升至109.05美元，是本次台灣股市休市期間最重要的壓力源。台積電ADR下跌0.72%傳遞了明確的壓力訊號，預示著台股開盤難以樂觀跳空，儘管夜盤台指期上漲，但富台指期下跌，多空訊息混雜。
【KOL與情緒】
社群熱詞顯示「川普談話」、「伊朗：正與阿曼起草荷姆茲海峽通行協議」、「烏克蘭打擊俄國阻俄油出口使油價上漲」等，皆指向地緣政治與油價是市場關注焦點，散戶情緒有轉向避險與投機的跡象。CNN恐懼貪婪指數已降至15，處於極度恐懼，而加密貨幣恐懼貪婪指數同為12，顯示市場整體偏向謹慎。
【操作含意】
考量到原油飆升、地緣政治緊張以及台積電ADR的下跌，下個交易日台股開盤預期偏向「低開或盤整」，操作上應保持謹慎，聚焦避險資產和具備真實轉強動能的標的，避免短線陷阱。觀察權值股如台積電、AI相關類股是否能守住3/2基準線之上將是關鍵。

sources: CTN News l Chiang Rai Times
evidence: Thailand Employs AI To Revolutionize The Payment Industry

news_themes.label: 馬來西亞海事專業人士面臨薪資與合約挑戰
news_themes.summary: 馬來西亞海員薪資遠低於國際標準，導致許多專業人士轉向外國船隻工作。合約制取代永久聘用、培訓費用上漲及稅務問題，都對海員的收入和職業發展造成負面影響。
evidence: 'Many jump ship for better wages'

news_themes.label: 川普言論對美元匯率的影響
news_themes.summary: 美國前總統川普的講話對美元匯率產生顯著影響，導致美元上下震盪，顯示了政治人物的言論在當前市場環境中的重要性。
evidence: 聚焦川普講話，美元上下震盪

chart_gap_suggestions.chart_name: 美股分析師共識圖 (Seeking Alpha)
chart_gap_suggestions.why_needed: 補上美股大型權值與科技股的分析師一致預期，讓報告不只看價格，也能看到評級與上行空間。
chart_gap_suggestions.evidence: 專案已有 Seeking Alpha service，可直接轉成圖表。

chart_gap_suggestions.why_needed: 把衝突事件數、致死數與未來風險預測可視化，讓地緣政治對市場的影響更直觀。
chart_gap_suggestions.evidence: 專案已有 ACLED service，但目前未做成可視化圖表。

chart_gap_suggestions.why_needed: 把 PTT 與 Dcard 的熱門討論、看多看空比與主題熱度做成圖，補足市場情緒面。
chart_gap_suggestions.evidence: 現有 trend_pulse 與社群抓取已能提供底層資料。

chart_gap_suggestions.why_needed: 晨報常聚焦 AI / 半導體 / 科技權值，將科技主題與新聞來源拆開呈現會更清楚。
chart_gap_suggestions.evidence: 目前科技內容散落在新聞主題與 AI 分析中，尚未獨立成圖。

headline.teaser: 美股週四小漲，但終場前急拉，顯示多頭仍有抵抗力。不過，台股休市期間，夜盤台指期收在 33,567，上漲 1.46%，富台指期則下跌 1.48%，多空訊息交錯，加上今晚美股即將開盤，需留意盤勢是否出現轉折。
